title: athyriopsis japonica
intro: Athyriopsis japonica, also known as the Japanese ostrich fern, is a type of fern native to Japan and other parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Athyriopsis japonica is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-tussive properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
